How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-08 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing on 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, exploiting signal convergence as the alpha source.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW dominates the batch at a score of 954, a structural outlier against a field where the next closest name, CAT, sits at 187. PANW's CLENOW_MOMENTUM plus PULLBACK plus VOLUME_LEADER combination reads as a momentum continuation with a buyable dip and confirmed participation. CAT and MS both carry CLENOW_MOMENTUM and PULLBACK, but CAT's EUPHORIA flag is a caution against chasing, while MS adds VARIANCE_PREMIUM, suggesting the options market is pricing in a directional move. The score distribution is sharply top-heavy: PANW is a singleton and the remaining nine names compress into a tight 95 to 187 band, and not one name in the batch hits a validated combo since matched_combo is null across the board, which materially caps conviction in a NEUTRAL macro regime scoring 45.98. PANW first at medium conviction, avoid CAT until the EUPHORIA signal clears.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: HUM (score 4, target ~$350.37 (DD20d -11.6%)).

1 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 1 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, filtered by EMA trend structure and low-OI anomaly to isolate likely new opening positions.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$707.41 -0.28%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape has only 5 resolved trades out of 295 total, so the 100% win rate and 200% average 20-day return versus SPY's 4.6% carry no statistical weight, and the monthly cohorts showing 100% rates with thousands-of-percent average option returns across April through June are equally inert at those sample sizes - the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe remain unconfirmed by live data.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 295 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 295 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

QQQ is the sole signal today at score 50, Watch tier, a BULLISH SWEEP flagged LONG - below both the 65 and 80 thresholds where this screener has historically added value. One name at the floor of the scoring range with zero signals at 65 or above makes this batch as thin as it gets. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (45.98), which does not actively support a broad-index sweep read. At score 50 with no corroborating signals anywhere in the batch, conviction is low - QQQ LONG is the only name on the sheet, and minimum size or a pass is the right call today.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest rather than contradicting it: 70.4% win rate and +3.19% average return versus SPY at -1.05%, a +4.2-point beat across 500 resolved trades, and monthly cohorts reinforce the trend with win rate climbing from 50.8% in April to 86% in June, the key number behind the KEEP verdict.

Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

APD and SNAP both print 100 scores at Very strong tier with LONG signals, joined by KMX at 99; pattern fields are null across the board, so the vol-coherence signal alone anchors every thesis here. The distribution is top-heavy: 25 of 50 names clear 80 and 43 of 50 clear 65, with a median of 79, meaning the screen is not scraping marginal signals today. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (45.98 as of 2026-07-08), a clean backdrop for this screen since it relies on vol structure rather than directional regime confirmation. Take APD first at maximum conviction given the tied 100 score and its less event-driven vol profile versus SNAP, which carries higher idiosyncratic noise risk even when VRP is coherent.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names score 100 today - ALL, BUG, CLFD, CRNX, ECL, FCNCA, FRHC, and GPC - all tagged Very strong LONG with no pattern data attached, which removes any within-screen tiebreaker. The distribution is severely compressed: median 97 across 50 signals, all 50 at or above 65 and 39 above 80, meaning the screen is running at the ceiling and offering almost no discrimination. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (45.98), so the regime is neither a headwind nor a clear assist to the contrarian thesis. Without pattern data or a differentiated score, the first calls are ALL and ECL as the larger-cap, more liquid representatives of the group, but conviction is low given the absence of any signal to break the tie.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the backtest - 67% live win rate versus 65% historical and +3.7% avg against SPY +0.6% earns a KEEP - but the monthly cohort trend partially contradicts the aggregate: April through June have run at 53-60% win rate, well below the backtest norm, with March's 67% win rate and +4.6% avg doing the heavy lifting and masking meaningful recent compression.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 67% · avg +3.7% · beats SPY +0.6% by +3.1 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 492 resolved at +20d · WR 67% · avg +3.7% · vs SPY +0.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 497 closed · WR TB 54% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AVNS is the clear lead at a perfect 100 score in the Very strong tier with a LAUNCH pattern, a rare max-score signal worth prioritizing above everything else in today's batch. EXPD and TFII both print 90 - Very strong, LAUNCH - and carry identical setup quality, making them logical secondary fills once AVNS has an entry. Below those three, JAZZ, MDB, NEU, NTAP, and NVRI cluster at 85 in the Strong tier with TRIGGER patterns; none differentiates on score alone and the tier-level 90d win rate of 60% makes them lower-conviction additions. The score distribution is top-heavy and concentrated: median sits at 80, 35 of 50 signals print at or above 80, and all 50 clear 65, which means the screen is filtering tightly today rather than spitting out marginal setups. Macro prints NEUTRAL at 45.98, neither a headwind nor a tailwind, so treat this as a stock-picker's environment and run AVNS first at high conviction.

🧠 The live tape confirms rather than contradicts the backtest, with 119 resolved trades printing a 68% win rate and +9.1% average against a SPY baseline of -0.6% for a +9.7 point edge, and the June 2026 cohort mirrors those numbers almost exactly at 68.7% and +9.1%, keeping the +11.25% 120d backtest claim credible.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LAC and PPTA share the top score at 94 in the Very strong tier, with LI and TMC close behind at 91 and BTU at 90, giving today five names that clear the highest bar before the list drops to Strong at 84. No pattern data is available for any pick, so the entry rationale is score and tier only. The distribution is compressed and uniformly elevated, with 21 of 23 signals printing 80 or above and a median of 83, which is top-heavy in a constructive way rather than thin. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 45.98 provides no directional tailwind, but this screener is designed as a basket trade with LEAPS, not a macro-momentum play, so regime friction is manageable. Preference is LAC first given the tied 94 score, structured as a basket LEAPS position rather than spot, at moderate conviction pending any pattern confirmation that is currently absent.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Flags vol-of-vol climax using VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to time sell-vol entries at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑62 Watch$743.65 -0.54%SELL VOL1.43
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Flags informed options flow via volume spikes above 5x the 20-day mean, filtered to calm non-extended tickers to remove chasing signals.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate across 18 resolved trades averaging +195.3% against SPY at -0.24%, and monthly cohorts from March through June echo unanimous win rates, but the screener's own verdict is WATCH given only 18 resolved of the 20 required, which means the backtest's 61.1% win rate and MARGINAL p-Bonferroni of 0.097 remain the only statistically credible anchor - recent results tease confirmation but do not yet deliver it.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMZU leads at score 72 and is the only Signal-tier name in today's batch, but it carries a direction/pattern conflict - the flag says LONG while the pattern reads BEARISH SWEEP - that contradiction needs resolution before sizing anything. HPE at 63 is the sole name at or above the 65 threshold and shows clean alignment between its LONG direction and BULLISH SWEEP, making it the most actionable read today. The remaining seven names cluster at the median of 54, nothing clears 80, and the batch is thin and bunched rather than top-heavy with conviction. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 45.98, providing no directional tailwind. HPE is the first look, low conviction, and AMZU gets no size until the direction flag is reconciled.

🧠 Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs by momentum strength, acceleration, and RS crossover vs SPY to flag regime transitions early.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLU, XLE, and XLC each score 95 (Very strong) today, a combination worth flagging: utilities and energy read as defensive rotation, while communications at the same score injects a growth signal that muddies the sector narrative. The distribution is top-heavy (4 at or above 80, median 65, cluster of Watch names at 55-60 doing nothing), and the absence of pattern classifications for every single pick removes the entry-timing precision the construction normally provides. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 45.98) gives no directional push, which means the three top-scoring names are riding their own momentum rather than a macro tailwind. First preference is XLE as the cleanest read in the group given energy's volatility tends to produce sharper momentum signals at score extremes; conviction is moderate, not high, until pattern context fills in.

🧠 Flags stocks down 40-plus percent from their 52-week high on capitulation volume with a reversal candle, scoring exhaustion severity to time mean-reversion entries.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking confirms the strategy directionally - 64% win rate and avg +7.6% beats SPY by 6.7 points, in line with the 63.3% backtest win rate - but the monthly cohorts reveal a Jan-Feb collapse to -12.9% avg that has only partially recovered, with May's 2,736-signal batch still averaging just +2.9%, making recent environment shallower than the aggregate headline suggests.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IVA prints a perfect 100 with ADCT, JFIN, LI, RGC, SOC, WW, and BTGO clustered at 95-90, every name carrying the same BREAKOUT pattern and Very Strong tier. The score distribution is top-heavy: 34 of 50 signals clear 80, the median is 81, and all 50 reach 65, so there is no thin tail to ignore. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 removes neither tailwind nor headwind, which is acceptable for a mean-reversion play. The critical problem is that the Very Strong tier has delivered only a 33% win rate in live 90-day tracking against Signal tier's 75% - today's entire batch sits in the historically weakest live-performance bucket. If forced to pick one, IVA at 100 leads, but conviction is low given the tier paradox; position sizing should reflect that.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores US equities by 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, filtered on price above MA100 and a SPY-above-MA200 regime gate.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Two signals is as thin as this screener gets. NKE scores 100 in the Very strong tier, but live data inverts that label: Very strong carries a 30% win rate and -11.87% average 20-day return, the worst performance of any tier in the 90-day window, so the top score is a caution flag rather than a green light. GIS scores 77 in the Signal tier, which actually runs 66.7% and +2.60% average in tracking, making it the more credible setup despite the lower score. The distribution is top-heavy with a hard gap between 100 and 77, one name above 80, and macro sitting at NEUTRAL (45.98) adding no directional push. GIS is the first look at minimal size, low conviction, and only because the tier inversion makes the 100-score name the weaker live bet.

🧠 Detects intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma, 7-day) across major pairs and fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more pairs dislocate simultaneously.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks with positive EPS surprises and gap-ups that hold, capturing delayed institutional repositioning and analyst revision lift over 1 to 3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$10.97 -1.14%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tracking at 59% WR and +4.2% avg over a near-flat SPY nominally confirms the edge, but the key number is June's 43% WR and +0.6% avg on 58 signals - a cohort-level collapse that contradicts the KEEP verdict and warns the drift trade is losing teeth entering the current month.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record at 75% WR and +5.0% avg beats SPY by 4.2 points and says keep running the screen, but May's cohort collapsed to 49% WR and -1.1% avg before June recovered to 64% WR and +3.0% - that May number is the one to watch.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals print at score 100 in Very strong tier - the distribution is fully compressed at the ceiling with zero differentiation within the batch. APLD, CI, and DIG are the names to sequence first, though null patterns across all picks mean score is the only variable and score is identical everywhere. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 45.98, which is neither fuel nor friction. Very strong tier in the 90-day data runs 78% WR and +5.2% avg, and every name today lands there rather than in the stronger-performing Strong tier (83% WR, +6.3% avg). Start with APLD at low conviction; this is not a high-confidence batch.

Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms rather than contradicts the screener - 57.9% WR and +4.6% avg vs. SPY at -0.7% earns a KEEP verdict, and the cohort recovery from April's 44.4% WR and -0.73% avg back to 57.3% and +4.2% by June-2026 reinforces that the edge is intact, with the +5.3-point SPY spread across 494 resolved trades as the key number.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC, ANAB, and BV all hit the ceiling at 95 and are flagged PRIMED, but the live tier data is a direct warning - Very strong names run only 31.8% win rate and avg -7.7% over the trailing 90 days, the worst result in the cohort by a wide margin. Today's batch loads 17 of 50 names at 90-plus, placing the bulk of weight in exactly the tier that has historically destroyed capital. The score distribution is compressed and top-heavy: all 50 signals clear 65, median sits at 75, but the cluster at the ceiling is where the edge inverts. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 is a non-factor for squeeze mechanics, which live and die on flow and borrow cost, not regime score. First preference is ABEO at 85 in the Strong tier, which runs 64.7% WR and +7.2% avg live, at moderate conviction only.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume times multi-horizon momentum to surface names where elevated turnover confirms directional price strength.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is extreme: all 50 signals score between 96 and 100 with a median of 96, giving the screener zero gradient to differentiate names. MRNA and KYMR at 100 stand out on the basis that biotech prints this kind of volume spike ahead of binary catalysts, which can produce sharp single-stock moves independent of the broader screen. JPM at 99 adds a large-cap financials read, but every pick falls in the 'Very strong' tier, which is the worst-performing tier in live tracking at -7.6% average and 37.4% WR. A neutral macro score of 45.98 neither supports nor disrupts momentum, but the screener's KILL verdict makes the entire batch structurally low-conviction. If forced to prioritize, MRNA first at minimal size, on the logic that a pending catalyst can override screen-level drag, not because the screen earns it.

🧠 Detects OTM options block buys over $200K on low-activity stocks, using next-day OI delta to confirm new institutional accumulation versus closing trades.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags Market Profile rejection tails at value-area extremes, betting that price rejected hard at the distribution edge will revert toward the value center.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GWX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GWX ↑100●●● Very strong$42.88 -1.54%LOW2.3210.9%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 5-trade live sample posts 80% win rate and +4.44% avg return at 20 days against a SPY baseline of -0.27%, and the June cohort improved on May (100% WR, +5.33% avg vs. 67% WR, +3.85%), both directionally consistent with the filtered backtest edge of +3.18%, but the WATCH verdict holds and the key number is 5 resolved against the 20 needed to move this off observation status.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings drift via the first weekly pullback inside a gap-up reaction, buying when price reclaims the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects smart money flow signals and discards any setup where KVO or Force Index flags a volume conflict within 3 days.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters

original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.

Fresh 52w high or pivot breakout after tight base contraction (ATR shrinking). Score uses breakout strength, volume confirmation, weekly trend, and SPY filter (require_spy_above_sma50).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BLZE, DFTX, and QLYS each score 80 and trigger the full TRIGGER pattern, making them the only three Strong-tier names in today's 50-signal batch and the clearest exhaustion setups the screener can produce. The distribution is mildly top-heavy: 3 names at the 80 ceiling, 29 of 50 at or above the 65 median, the rest spread thin below. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 45.98 gives no directional lean for shorts. The screener is backtested-rejected and tracking-killed, so even the cleanest TRIGGER flags carry low conviction. BLZE is the first look given score parity with the other two, but size should reflect a dead screener.

🧠 Weekly RSI(14) below 30 exhaustion gate, confirmed by daily crossover and candlestick pattern, targeting a mean-reversion long bounce.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $LXU $DKL $BORR

9EOG ↑53 Watch$136.59 +1.52%18.42—NOENGULFING
10LYB ↑52 Watch$55.81 +2.22%17.8323.59NOHAMMER
11CNR ↑50 Watch$80.73 +1.98%37.9020.62YESNONE
12COP ↑50 Watch$110.53 +1.92%40.9128.23YESNONE
13DDL ↑50 Watch$2.02 -1.23%32.2625.64YESNONE
14NRP ↑50 Watch$97.76 -0.11%31.5920.08YESNONE

16ROL ↑50 Watch$43.95 +0.76%33.1624.55YESNONE
17WLK ↑50 Watch$78.17 +2.50%33.2421.33YESNONE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record - 54% win rate and +4.1% avg vs SPY at -0.6% - supports real-world edge despite the formal backtest rejection, but the monthly cohort trend cuts against it: win rate has deteriorated from 78.7% in March to 52.75% in June and May's average return was -0.44%, signaling the edge is fading in the current tape.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LXU at 67 and DKL at 66 are the only two Signal-tier names, both printing SETUP patterns, making them the clearest entries in an otherwise thin batch. BORR and FANG score 63 and 61 as Watch-tier SETUP names, but four of the top eight are energy-related, so the batch reads as a sector cluster rather than a broad oversold opportunity. The score distribution confirms the weakness: 17 signals, median 53, zero names above 80, and only two clearing 65 - this is a compressed, low-conviction batch with no standout score. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 neither supports nor undermines a contrarian RSI reversal, but it removes the tailwind a risk-on regime would provide. Take LXU first on its marginal score edge, size it small, and treat the entire batch as provisional until the tape confirms.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and open-interest shifts in crypto-linked ETFs, scoring flow intensity weighted against spot crypto correlation as the directional signal.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $HUT $BITO $FBTC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1HUT ↑75● Signal$100.24 +3.62%41.23-12.3%neutral
2BITO ↑50 Watch$8.40 -2.83%44.00-0.5%neutral
3FBTC ↑50 Watch$53.91 -2.92%44.68-0.2%neutral
4IBIT ↑50 Watch$35.09 -2.92%44.550.0%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IDNA leads the batch at 70, the only name at the screener's ceiling, sitting inside the genomics theme alongside ARKG at 68 - both flagged long with no pattern detail available. VHT at 69 adds broader healthcare exposure, and the top eight effectively cluster around health, infrastructure, staples, and utilities, with NFRA (67), VDC (67), XLV (66), WCLD (65), and VPU (65) filling out the rest. Score distribution is compressed and top-heavy but not strong: zero signals clear 80, the max is 70, and the median sits at 56, so only 11 of 50 names show any real conviction. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 does not actively support theme momentum plays, making this a low-tailwind environment for the screener's thesis. First look goes to IDNA at low-to-medium conviction, the sole score-ceiling name in a batch where that ceiling is itself modest.

🧠 The 90-day live record, 64% win rate and avg +7.5% beating SPY by 8.4 points across 135 resolved trades, confirms the screener carries real edge, though the sole monthly cohort (May 2026, avg 18.69%) is skewed by a single 1385% outlier and no formal backtest has run yet, so the edge case rests entirely on live tracking.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +7.5% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.4 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 135 resolved at +20d · WR 64% · avg +7.5% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 35% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers hit score 100 and Very strong today, including BL (enterprise software), CELH (beverage consumer), and SRTY (3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF), but pattern data is null across the board, leaving only the raw score to work with. SRTY flagged long as a short ETF is the most counterintuitive signal in the batch and requires an independent thesis before touching it. Score distribution across the 50-signal pool is top-heavy: median 70, 41 of 50 at 65 or above, 12 at 80 or above, and today's 8 perfect-score names form a real cluster at the ceiling. Macro at NEUTRAL (45.98) provides no directional lift, and the Very strong tier has historically averaged only 3.43% at 20 days, well below the Watch tier's 9.54%, which tempers conviction on the highest-scored names. Lead with CELH at reduced size, pass on SRTY without a separate catalyst.

🧠 Scores stocks by breadth of institutional accumulation across 20 top funds, with new positions weighted highest, using quarterly 13F filings.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names clearing the Very Strong tier, both flagging broad new-position or heavy-add activity across the whale universe. AVGO at 79 is next but sits in Signal territory, a meaningful step down. The distribution is thin at the top: two names above 80 out of 50 signals, median at 55, which means the bulk of this batch is low-conviction filler. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 45.98 provides no directional tailwind to amplify these reads, making selectivity more important than breadth here. ASML first, medium conviction, with the hard caveat that every 13F signal carries a 60-90 day structural lag by construction.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and COE both score 100, the ceiling of today's 45-name batch, with JEF at 98 standing out as the only recognizable large-cap name and the only pick offering meaningful secondary-market liquidity. The score distribution is fully compressed, every one of the 45 signals lands at 80 or above and the median sits at 95, meaning the ranking system produces near-zero differentiation across this batch and the scores themselves carry little relative information. No pattern data is attached to any pick, stripping out any technical confirmation layer. The macro regime reads NEUTRAL at 45.98, providing no directional tailwind, so the entire thesis rests on the insider signal alone. Take JEF first at moderate conviction on liquidity grounds, then SVRE if the float can absorb the size.

🧠 Identifies individual stocks lagging their already-moving sector, scoring catch-up probability via sector relative strength times stock underperformance.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 22 signals land at the score floor of 50, with zero names reaching 65 and zero reaching 80 - the distribution is maximally compressed and provides no differentiation whatsoever. The top names, ORA, OKLO, and CWEN, all carry identical Watch-tier scores of 50 with no pattern data recorded, so the screener itself is not ranking them against each other. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 is not the backdrop where sector catch-up trades run cleanly - this mechanism needs a sector already in gear with momentum spillover, not a directionless tape. Today's batch is weak and should not be acted on. If forced to pick one name on an exogenous fundamental overlay, OKLO sits in the nuclear energy sector narrative, but that is your call to make, not the screener's.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending so the cheapest operating earners score 100, targeting deep-value mean reversion in names ignored by growth-oriented capital.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape has zero resolved trades against a 20-trade minimum so the WATCH verdict reflects a data gap rather than a negative signal, with the only available number being a 61.54% win rate across 26 tracking observations, which is directionally encouraging but far too thin to confirm or contradict the as-yet-unrun backtest.

BZ leads the batch at a perfect 100 with Very Strong tier, and WRD at 97 sits just behind it as the two clearest expressions of the multiple at its extreme. GPC at 90 is notable given its size and consistency as an auto-parts distributor, rarely this cheap on operating earnings. The score distribution is top-heavy, with 7 of 16 signals printing at or above 80 and the median at 73, so this is not a thin or noisy batch. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 45.98, an indifferent backdrop for deep value since the factor demands either a reflation catalyst or a risk-on rotation to convert cheapness into price, and neither is visible here. Start with BZ at moderate conviction given the max score, size small until macro tilts toward risk-on.

🧠 Flags stocks where 3+ insiders buy within 30 days, scored by cluster size, dollar value, rank, and recency to surface informed accumulation.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape at WR 60% and avg +5.0% against SPY at -0.3% confirms live signal, but the monthly WR trend from April (44%) to May (62%) to June (56%) is uneven, and the April average of +20.8% is outlier-driven by a single 539% return, making May at WR 62% and avg +4.7% the cleanest read that the edge is real and roughly consistent.

The NEUTRAL macro regime at 45.98 is not a tailwind for momentum longs, and the entire batch is concentrated in semiconductors and networking hardware, so a sector-level move drives most of the dispersion here rather than idiosyncratic stock selection. 🧠 Identifies top relative-strength stocks within leading sectors, combining 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster ranking to surface rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists yet (N=0), so the live tape stands alone: 55.4% WR and avg +4.3% across 175 resolved trades beats SPY by 6.1 points, and the single May 2026 cohort (WR 55.7%, avg +10.8%) supports KEEP but gives no multi-month trend to read.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Five signals today, the thinnest output this screener produces. AAT leads at score 82 - the only name above 80 - but falls in Strong tier, which tracks at just 47.8% WR and avg +1.9% over 90 days. AAMI (76) and ABCL (74) both land in Signal tier, the best-performing cohort in live tracking at 72% WR and avg +9.2%, making tier a more useful filter than raw score today. The distribution is narrow and low-conviction, clustered from 56 to 82 with a median of 74, and macro sits at NEUTRAL (45.98), providing no sector-rotation tailwind. No patterns fire on any name and ABCB at 56/Watch offers no edge; if sizing anything, AAMI at 76/Signal is the first choice at low conviction, favored over the higher-scored AAT precisely because Signal tier dramatically outperforms Strong tier in live data.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings straddles where IV underprices Yang-Zhang HV20, identifying events where the market underestimates the likely realized move.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $WFC $DHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑65● Signal$335.31 -1.15%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2WFC ↑65● Signal$86.39 -0.91%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3DHR ↑55 Watch$191.62 -1.32%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
4GE ↑55 Watch$361.50 -1.49%PRE_IV_RAMP9—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

JPM and WFC are the only Signal-tier names, both scoring 65 - the ceiling of today's batch - flagged as long straddle plays into their earnings windows with no pattern data attached to either. DHR and GE sit at 55, Watch only, adding little conviction. The distribution is compressed and weak: four total signals, zero clearing 80, median at 55, top of range at 65 - this is a thin, low-confidence batch by any measure. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 provides no regime tailwind for long vol positioning. JPM is the first preference on liquidity grounds alone, but the score ceiling of 65 and absent pattern context keep conviction low.

SPY at 80 is the lone Strong-tier name and the only one clearing the median, but it prints alongside a bullish VXX at 75 - a pairing that conflicts, since VXX profits from rising volatility, signaling hedged uncertainty rather than clean directionality. Score distribution is compressed: all four signals land at or above 65, three cluster at exactly 70, and pattern data is null across every name, which strips out a layer of confirmation the screener normally provides. NEUTRAL macro at 45.98 does not support chasing high-beta, and RIVN at 70 carries the most regime risk in this batch. HPE at 70 is the cleanest single-name expression, operationally less macro-sensitive and not entangled in the SPY/VXX tension. Start there at moderate conviction, sizing light until pattern fields populate.

🧠 Identifies parabolic, catalyst-free retail melt-ups by combining social mention z-score, call/put ratio, and price stretch to flag reversal candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Catches bear/bull traps at Volume Area extremes, flagging VAL undercut-then-reclaim or VAH overrun-then-reject as trapped-position unwinds.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AMTM $ACM $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AMTM ↑65● Signal$20.71 -2.22%BEAR_TRAP20.9223.040.89
2ACM ↑60 Watch$66.56 -1.60%BEAR_TRAP66.8270.352.35
3AVGO ↑60 Watch$379.80 +2.43%BEAR_TRAP366.64382.4218.16
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at WR 76%, avg +6.2%, beating SPY by +7.1 pts, with monthly cohorts holding at June 73.9% WR / +5.0% and May 76.9% / +8.0%, is the only statistical basis for running this screener at all, since the formal backtest remains at N=0 and unvalidated - the live tracking confirms direction but cannot substitute for a rigorous test.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV exceeds call IV above the 90th percentile, identifying concentrated, potentially directional put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$379.80 +2.43%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest to compare against, the 90-day record of 9 trades (55.6% win rate, -5.7% average 20-day return vs. SPY at +0.77%) neither confirms nor contradicts edge, but the tier split is the most instructive detail available: Very strong names (AVGO's bucket) post 75% win rate and +8.0% average 20-day return while the Strong bucket delivers 0% and -55.9%, a divide too wide to ignore once sample size grows past the current 9-trade floor.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags individual names where IV30 minus HV20 spikes above 1.5 standard deviations on 90 days, surfacing inflated fear premium as volatility-selling candidates.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $ALNY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1ALNY ↓62 Watch$319.40 -0.68%50%32%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The monthly cohort trend is the key number to watch and it is deteriorating: April ran 55.9% WR at +10.8% avg, May slipped to 42% WR at -0.22%, and June so far sits at 34% WR and -3.3% avg, a sequential collapse that directly contradicts the 90-day composite read of 43% WR and +1.5% avg over SPY, and suggests signal quality has degraded materially in recent weeks.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline and fades hedging panic as a contrarian long when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑75● Signal$94.23 -3.21%66%1000.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms a real edge - 54% WR, avg +8.1% against SPY's +1.2% - but the key anomaly is a hard tier inversion where Very Strong signals post -5.9% average and 12.5% WR while Watch-tier names post +14.75% and 68% WR, and the June cohort at -1.9% on only 10 names is the softest month in the window, meaning the screener's highest-conviction labels and the most recent entries are its weakest performers.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies a fake breakout above VAH that closes back inside the value area, trapping longs and generating a short signal on the expected exit flush.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ATEC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ATEC ↓60 Watch$8.98 -4.21%BULL_TRAP9.367.46
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtested statistics exist at N=0 trades, so the live tape is the only arbiter, and the key number is the options basket win rate of 35.2% across 54 contracts, a figure that confirms KILL even as June's equity-side win rate ticked up to 59.3% from May's 33.3%.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional flow signals to flag options edge.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$22.06 +1.71%53%854%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape directly contradicts any usable edge: 27 resolved trades at 26% win rate and -9.2% avg return versus SPY -0.4% earns a KILL verdict, and the monthly cohorts - April at -24.6% avg on one trade, May at 25% win rate and -9.1% avg on 24 trades - confirm the signal has been consistently destructive, with June's apparent 50% win rate resting on a sample of two and an avg still negative at -2.3%.

🧠 Screens equities using Altman Z-score components to flag solvency inflection points where distress or recovery creates a pricing dislocation.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags price-spread and volume relationships that suggest institutional accumulation or distribution at key turning points.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
